description_long: This object is a mature coconut that has been split into two halves, revealing the fibrous, brown outer husk and the white, edible inner flesh. The exterior is covered in a dense layer of coarse, brown fibers that are irregularly distributed, giving it a rough and natural appearance. The interior of the coconut is smooth and white, contrasting sharply with the dark, textured shell. One half of the coconut is fully intact, while the other half is open, exposing the hollow cavity and the thickness of the flesh. The object appears realistic, with visible fiber strands protruding from the shell and a slightly uneven break along the open edge. There are no visible markings, labels, or artificial modifications, and the coconut is presented against a plain background.
description: A split coconut with a rough, fibrous brown shell and smooth white flesh, showing both the intact and open halves. The texture and color contrast between the husk and the interior are clearly visible.
description_view_0: The coconut is shown with both halves visible, the open half facing forward to display the white flesh and hollow interior.
description_view_1: The view highlights the rounded, fibrous exterior of the intact half, with the open half partially visible behind it.
description_view_2: The intact half is prominent, with the open half positioned behind, showing the thickness of the shell and flesh.
description_view_3: Both halves are visible, with the open sides facing outward, clearly displaying the white interior and the rough shell edges.
attribute_husk_pattern: The outer husk is densely covered with coarse, brown fibers that are irregularly oriented, with some fibers sticking out in various directions. The fiber density is high, especially near the top and bottom, and the surface appears uneven with visible striations and patches of varying thickness.
attribute_stem_attachment: A small, circular stem scar is visible at the top of the intact half, slightly indented and surrounded by denser fibers.
